title: Herb Silk Fish
description: A silky and herbal brunch-mood Australian dish featuring fish fillet with a dash-friendly touch.

ingredients.name: Fish Fillet
ingredients.notes: Choose a firm white fish like Cod or Snapper

ingredients.name: Chopped Fresh Herbs
ingredients.amount: a handful of mixed herbs such as parsley, dill, chervil, and tarragon

ingredients.name: Melted Vegan Butter or Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Can be replaced with a vegan substitute if needed

ingredients.name: Squeeze of Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Avocado Mash (for garnish)
ingredients.notes: Optional but adds a silky texture

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Pat the fish fillets dry with paper towels.
In a mixing bowl, combine chopped fresh herbs, melted vegan butter or olive oil, lemon juice, and salt and pepper. Mix well until evenly distributed.
Coat both sides of the fish fillets generously with the herb mixture. Place them in an oven-safe dish lined with baking parchment.
Bake for 10-12 minutes at 350°F (175°C) or until the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork.
While the fish is cooking, prepare the avocado mash by mashing an avocado with salt and pepper. Set aside as a garnish.
Once done, serve the herb-silk-fish fillets on plates with the avocado mash for extra texture and flavor.
